programme.title: The 1951 Festival of Britain: Brave New World
programme.desc: Documentary telling the story of the 1951 Festival of Britain, which in a period of austerity showed how to carve out a bright new future through design and ingenuity.

programme.title: Art That Made Us
programme.desc: A splintering of politics and religion under the Stuarts leads to more questioning art. Contains scenes which some viewers may find upsetting.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm.

programme.title: The Yorkshire Dales
programme.desc: Paul Rose heads underground in Swaledale to try and find rare industrial artefacts.

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Life on England's waterways with Robbie Cumming. Robbie gets stuck in the mud in Woodseaves Cutting and explores the charming canal-side village of Kinver in Staffordshire.

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Sitcom. Hyacinth and Richard go to join the Major at a hotel for a golfing weekend. The Major cries off playing, but has organised a friend to play with Richard.

programme.title: Sorry!
programme.desc: Timothy is suffering from bouts of sleepwalking, which could cause trouble as the annual interview for promotion is just around the corner.

programme.title: A History of Britain by Simon Schama
programme.desc: Simon Schama looks beyond the romantic stories of Cavaliers and Roundheads to the real story of the English Civil War, in which hundreds of thousands died.

programme.title: Dogs of War - Storyville
programme.desc: Film about a seemingly ordinary Englishman who spent more than 40 years as a mercenary. Contains strong language and some upsetting scenes.

programme.title: The Empire State Story - Timewatch
programme.desc: First transmitted in 2001, this programme chronicles the construction of the Empire State Building in New York, which was the world's tallest skyscraper when opened in 1931.

programme.title: The Jet Stream and Us
programme.desc: Documentary tracing how understanding of the powerful and changing weather phenomenon that is the jet stream - a ribbon of fast-moving air high in the atmosphere - has grown.

programme.title: Barcelona: An Art Lovers' Guide
programme.desc: Janina Ramirez and Alastair Sooke discover Barcelona through its art and architecture, including the frescoes that inspired Miro and a surprising collection of vintage fans.

programme.title: Lost Cities of the Ancients
programme.desc: The documentary series which unearths lost cities looks at the mystery surrounding the Egyptian city of Piramesse, built by Ramesses the Great.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm.

programme.title: The Yorkshire Dales
programme.desc: Explorer Paul Rose's Yorkshire Dales adventure concludes in Wharfedale. He meets the Calendar Girls and finds out why bats are threatened in the Dales.

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries: Stourport Basins to Kingswood Junction
programme.desc: Robbie navigates the mighty River Severn and takes an unexpected bath.

programme.title: The Lost Neanderthals
programme.desc: Scientists uncover the secrets of the Mandrin Cave in France, where the remains of a Neanderthal were found in 2015. Contains some scenes which some viewers may find upsetting.

programme.title: Inside Chernobyl's Mega Tomb
programme.desc: Documentary which follows the construction of a trailblazing 36,000-tonne steel structure to entomb the ruins of the nuclear power plant destroyed in the 1986 Chernobyl disaster.

programme.title: Edna the Inebriate Woman - Play for Today
programme.desc: Patrica Hayes stars as Edna, an alcoholic homeless woman in search of shelter. Contains some scenes which some viewers may find upsetting.

programme.title: How to Get Ahead: At Medieval Court
programme.desc: Stephen Smith finds out what it took to get ahead at the court of Richard II.

programme.title: The Lost Neanderthals
programme.desc: Scientists uncover the secrets of the Mandrin Cave in France, where the remains of a Neanderthal were found in 2015. Contains some scenes which some viewers may find upsetting.

programme.title: The Empire State Story - Timewatch
programme.desc: First transmitted in 2001, this programme chronicles the construction of the Empire State Building in New York, which was the world's tallest skyscraper when opened in 1931.

programme.title: Inside Chernobyl's Mega Tomb
programme.desc: Documentary which follows the construction of a trailblazing 36,000-tonne steel structure to entomb the ruins of the nuclear power plant destroyed in the 1986 Chernobyl disaster.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm.

programme.title: Pubs, Ponds and Power: The Story of the Village
programme.desc: Ben Robinson unlocks the ancient roots of the Northumberland village of Warkworth, discovering clues that point back almost 1,000 years to the Norman conquest.

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries: Stratford-upon-Avon to Birmingham
programme.desc: Robbie crosses an aqueduct and gets stuck in a lock.

programme.title: Murder on the Orient Express
programme.desc: Returning to England from Istanbul on the Orient Express, Hercule Poirot is asked to investigate a murder.

programme.title: Albert Finney: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A retrospective look at television appearances made over the years by award-winning actor Albert Finney, with archive interviews and classic clips. Narrated by Sylvia Syms.

programme.title: The Importance of Being Earnest
programme.desc: Adaptation of Oscar Wilde's masterpiece, set in 1890s England. A comedy of mistaken identity ensues when two gentlemen friends use the same alias to pursue the women they love.

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Sun
programme.desc: Kate Humble and Helen Czerski reveal the inner workings of the sun and investigate why scientists think changes in the sun's behaviour may have powerful effects on our climate.

programme.title: The Riviera: A History in Pictures
programme.desc: Richard E Grant explores how modern art and the Riviera grew up together when the Cote d'Azur became both a hedonistic playground and an experimental studio.

programme.title: The Jet Stream and Us
programme.desc: Documentary tracing how understanding of the powerful and changing weather phenomenon that is the jet stream - a ribbon of fast-moving air high in the atmosphere - has grown.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm.

programme.title: TOTP: 1999
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 24 September 1999 and featuring Vengaboys, Everything but the Girl, Paul Johnson, David Bowie and Eiffel 65.

programme.title: TOTP: 1999
programme.desc: Jayne Middlemiss presents the pop chart programme from Ikon, Newcastle, first broadcast on 1 October 1999 and featuring DJ Jean, Kelle Bryan, S Club 7 and Eiffel 65.

programme.title: TOTP: 1988
programme.desc: Gary Davies and Nicky Campbell present the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 28 April 1988 and featuring Pat and Mick, James Brown, Fairground Attraction, The Primitives and Bananarama.

programme.title: TOTP: 1991
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 25 April 1991 and featuring EMF, Vic Reeves and Electronic.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty is busy this week as the garden bursts into colour.

programme.title: Dusty at the BBC
programme.desc: A second chance to explore the BBC's archives with one of British music's biggest and best-loved stars, Dusty Springfield.

programme.title: Dusty at the BBC
programme.desc: A selection of Dusty Springfield's performances at the BBC from 1961 to 1995, from her folk beginnings with The Springfields through to her collaboration with Pet Shop Boys.
